title: Organizing Cluster Access Using kubeconfig Files — The KUBECONFIG environment variable
text:
The
KUBECONFIG
environment variable holds a list of kubeconfig files.
For Linux and Mac, the list is colon-delimited. For Windows, the list
is semicolon-delimited. The
KUBECONFIG
environment variable is not
required. If the
KUBECONFIG
environment variable doesn't exist,
kubectl
uses the default kubeconfig file,
$HOME/.kube/config
.
If the
KUBECONFIG
environment variable does exist,
kubectl
uses
an effective configuration that is the result of merging the files
listed in the
KUBECONFIG
environment variable.
